components.name: Sodium chloride (NaCl)
components.evidence: ASW medium (Wilson et al. 1996) media-comparison table, ASW column: 'NaCl ... 25' g/L (footnote 5 Wilson et al. (1996) J. Phycol 32, 506-516)

components.name: Magnesium chloride (MgCl2·6H2O)
components.evidence: ASW column: 'MgCl2 · 7 H2O ... 2' g/L (Wilson et al. 1996; hydrate as printed in the culturing-manual table, commonly hexahydrate)

components.name: Magnesium sulfate (MgSO4·7H2O)
components.evidence: ASW column: 'MgSO4 · 6 H2O ... 3.5' g/L (Wilson et al. 1996; hydrate as printed, commonly heptahydrate)

components.name: Potassium chloride (KCl)
components.evidence: ASW column: 'KCl ... 0.5' g/L (Wilson et al. 1996)

components.name: Calcium chloride (CaCl2)
components.evidence: ASW column: 'CaCl2 ... 0.5' g/L (Wilson et al. 1996)

components.name: Tris base
components.evidence: ASW column, Buffers: 'TRIS-base, pH 8 (mM) ... 9.08' (Wilson et al. 1996)

components.name: Ammonium chloride (NH4Cl)
components.evidence: Modified ASW medium contained 10 mM sodium succinate as a carbon source, vitamins [1 ml liter−1 of marine ammonium mineral salts (MAMS) vitamins], and 8.8 mM ammonium chloride. (replaces the 8.8 mM NaNO3 of the base ASW)

components.name: Sodium succinate
components.evidence: Modified ASW medium contained 10 mM sodium succinate as a carbon source

components.name: Dipotassium phosphate (K2HPO4, Pi)
components.evidence: modified artificial seawater (ASW) medium (61) with reduced P concentration (50 μM final concentration) ... inoculated into modified ASW containing either PC ..., PE ..., K2HPO4 (Pi), or no P (−P control) ... final concentrations of P supplements ... varying between 50 μM and 1.73 mM

components.name: Phosphocholine (PC)
components.evidence: prestarved Phaeobacter cultures were inoculated into modified ASW containing either PC (CAS no. 72556-74-2; Sigma-Aldrich) ... Different final concentrations of P supplements were used, varying between 50 μM and 1.73 mM

components.name: Phosphorylethanolamine (PE)
components.evidence: modified ASW containing either PC ..., PE (CAS no. 1071-23-4; Sigma-Aldrich), K2HPO4 (Pi), or no P (−P control)

components.name: EDTA (Na2EDTA)
components.evidence: ASW column, Trace Metals (μM): 'EDTA ... 1.3' (Wilson et al. 1996)

components.evidence: ASW column, Trace Metals (μM): 'H3BO3 ... 46.3' (Wilson et al. 1996)

components.evidence: ASW column, Trace Metals (μM): 'Na2MoO4 · 2 H2O ... 1.61' (Wilson et al. 1996)

components.name: Copper sulfate (CuSO4·5H2O)
components.evidence: ASW column, Trace Metals (μM): 'CuSO4 · 5 H2O ... 0.03' (Wilson et al. 1996)

components.name: Cobalt nitrate (Co(NO3)2·6H2O)
components.evidence: ASW column, Trace Metals (μM): 'Co(NO3)2 · 6 H2O ... 0.17' (Wilson et al. 1996)

components.evidence: MAMS vitamins (1 ml/L) [Chen 2012], final concentrations reproduced in marine-Roseobacter methods: '30 nM thiamine, 160 nM nicotinic acid, 97 nM pyridoxine, 73 nM aminobenzoic acid, 53 nM riboflavin, 84 nM pantothenate, 4.1 nM biotin, 1.5 nM cyanocobalamin, and 11 nM folic acid'

components.evidence: '... 1.5 nM cyanocobalamin ...' (MAMS vitamins, Chen 2012, added 1 ml/L)

components.name: O2 (dioxygen)
components.evidence: Phaeobacter was grown in marine broth (Difco...) at 30°C with shaking (150 rpm) ... Cultures were then starved ... at 30°C with shaking at 140 rpm

media.base_medium: Artificial seawater (ASW) medium of Wilson, Carr & Mann 1996 (J. Phycol. 32:506-516), modified: NaNO3 replaced by 8.8 mM NH4Cl, 10 mM sodium succinate added as carbon source, MAMS vitamins (Chen 2012) added at 1 ml/L, and P reduced/varied (50 uM-1.73 mM) supplied as K2HPO4, phosphocholine or phosphorylethanolamine.

sources_chased.what: base ASW recipe cited as ref 61 (Wilson, Carr & Mann 1996, J. Phycol. 32:506-516)
sources_chased.how: WebFetch/Read of the Chisholm-lab (MIT) Prochlorococcus & Synechococcus culturing manual media-comparison table, whose ASW column is explicitly footnoted 'Wilson et al. (1996) J. Phycol 32, 506-516'

sources_chased.what: MAMS vitamins solution cited as ref 62 (Chen 2012, Environ. Microbiol. 14:2308-2322)
sources_chased.how: WebFetch of marine-Roseobacter methods (PMC8319176 and DSMZ medium 1313 / PMC4331573) that reproduce the Chen-2012 MAMS medium and its 1 ml/L vitamin solution

media.notes: Base is the Wilson-Carr-Mann 1996 ASW; the paper modifies it by supplying carbon (10 mM Na-succinate), N as 8.8 mM NH4Cl (equimolar substitution for the base ASW's 8.8 mM NaNO3, so nitrate is treated as replaced) and vitamins via 1 ml/L MAMS solution. P is the experimental variable (50 uM-1.73 mM) supplied as K2HPO4, phosphocholine (PC) or phosphorylethanolamine (PE), with a -P control. Grown aerobically (30 C, shaking); no reductant/anaerobic gas. Mo and B trace metals and all nine MAMS vitamins were absent from the prior component list and are the main recovered additions; hydrate forms for MgCl2/MgSO4 are printed as 7H2O/6H2O in the source table (commonly 6H2O/7H2O).
